final_answer:
Based on the information provided in the Wikipedia page, the United States won the most gold medals at the 2020 Summer Olympics, with 39 gold medals. Additionally, the United States won a total of 113 medals across all three categories (gold, silver, and bronze).

Therefore, the country that won the most gold medals at the 2020 Summer Olympics is the United States, and they won a total of 113 medals.

Part of a series on 2020 Summer Olympics Bid process ( bid details ) Development ( venues , torch relay ) Marketing ( mascots , pictograms ) Broadcasters Opening ceremony ( flag bearers ) Event calendar Chronological summary Medal table ( medalists ) Controversies ( Russian doping scandal , withdrawals , COVID-19 cases , Belarus scandal ) World and Olympic records Closing ceremony ( flag bearers ) Paralympics ( medal table ) IOC JOC TOCOG v t e The 2020 Summer Olympics , officially known as the Games of the XXXII Olympiad, were an international multi-sport event held in Tokyo , Japan, from 23 July to 8 August 2021. The Games were postponed by one year as part of the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on sports . [ 2 ] However, the Games was referred to by its original date in all medals, uniforms, promotional items, and other related media in order to avoid confusion in future years. [ 3 ] [ 4 ] A total of 11,417 athletes from 206 nations participated in 339 medal events in 33 sports across 50 disciplines. [ 5 ] Overall, the event saw two records: 93 nations received at least one medal, and 65 of them won at least one gold medal. [ 6 ] [ 7 ] Athletes from the United States won the most medals overall, with 113, and the most gold medals, with 39. [ 8 ] Host nation Japan won 27 gold medals, surpassing its gold medal tally of 16 at both the 1964 and 2004 su
